# Contributing to the Knowledge Graph

Notes for anyone editing graph data, build scripts, or tests. Save you the foot-guns we already stepped on.

---

## Layer layout

| Layer | Path | Source |
|---|---|---|
| code | `data/MentorAgent/code/code-graph.json` | Auto-generated by `code/extract.ps1` (do NOT hand-edit) |
| system | `data/MentorAgent/system/mentor-graph.json` | Hand-curated nodes & edges |
| merged | `output/merged-graph.json` | Built by `build/core/merge.ps1` |

## Edge wiring gotcha — read this before adding `tested_by` edges

WHEN manually wiring an edge type in the **system layer** → FIRST check if the **code-layer extractor** already emits it.

The code extractor auto-derives `tested_by` edges from test file naming conventions. If you also wire the edge in the system layer, the merge bridge produces duplicates.

- **Symptom:** Health check shows `[WARN] duplicate-edges` after rebuild.
- **Fix:** Dedupe pass on the system layer keyed on `source|target|type`:
  ```powershell
  $f = '.github/knowledge-graph/data/MentorAgent/system/mentor-graph.json'
  $g = Get-Content $f -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
  $seen = @{}
  $g.edges = @($g.edges | Where-Object {
      $k = "$($_.source)|$($_.target)|$($_.type)"
      if ($seen[$k]) { $false } else { $seen[$k] = $true; $true }
  })
  ($g | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100) | Set-Content $f -Encoding utf8
  ```

## Path resolution in `tests/` scripts

`$PSScriptRoot\..\..` from `.github/knowledge-graph/tests/` lands at `.github/`, NOT the repo root. Easy to get wrong.

Use both anchors:
```powershell
$kgRoot   = (Resolve-Path "$PSScriptRoot\..").Path        # for graph layer paths
$repoRoot = (Resolve-Path "$PSScriptRoot\..\..\..").Path  # for repo-relative file checks
```

Mirror the layer switch from `build/core/health.ps1` when resolving `code|system|merged` paths.

## Pre-commit gate behavior

`.github/hooks/pre-commit.ps1` is **strict** — blocks on islands and drift.

- Auto-rebuilds the code graph from staged changes.
- Auto-stages the regenerated `code-graph.json` and `mentor-graph.json`.
- Your commit will silently include refreshed graph files — that is expected.

## Test gauntlet

All five must exit 0 before pushing:

```powershell
$tests = @(
  '.github/knowledge-graph/tests/test-graph.ps1',
  '.github/knowledge-graph/cli/tests/test-e2e.ps1',
  '.github/knowledge-graph/cli/tests/test-scaffold.ps1',
  '.github/knowledge-graph/cli/tests/test-graph-writer.ps1',
  '.github/knowledge-graph/cli/tests/test-agent-sync.ps1'
)
foreach ($t in $tests) {
  pwsh -NoProfile -File $t *> $null
  Write-Host ("{0,-60} exit {1}" -f $t, $LASTEXITCODE)
}
```

Use `pwsh -NoProfile -File <path>` for clean exit codes — running a script directly in the integrated terminal can pick up cached/stale state.

## Commit message handling

PowerShell's multi `-m` flag corrupts commit messages. Use a temp file:

```powershell
# Write the message
@'
fix: short summary

Longer description with multiple lines.
'@ | Set-Content .tmp-commit-msg.txt -Encoding utf8

git commit -F .tmp-commit-msg.txt
Remove-Item .tmp-commit-msg.txt
```
